text: One of the main goals of implementation science is the identification, development, and evaluation of implementation strategies. Work in this area has been held up by variations in terminology and inadequate description of the various strategies in the literature. The Expert Recommendations for Implementing Change (ERIC) study was conducted to enhance and refine the vocabulary and definitions of implementation terms. Through soliciting input from both implementation scientists and practice providers, ERIC has resulted in a dictionary of terms about implementation strategies that is both theoretically sound and clinically relevant.
